data.title: BofA’s Kamdem Reiterates Buy on American Healthcare REIT, Citing Leadership Stability and Strategic Continuity
data.description:
Ronald Kamdem, an analyst from Morgan Stanley, maintained the Buy rating on American Healthcare REIT, Inc.. The associated price target is $55.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Ronald Kamdem has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to leadership stability and strategic continuity at American Healthcare REIT, Inc. The elevation of co‑founder Jeff Hanson from interim to permanent CEO, alongside his ongoing role as Chairman, reinforces a consistent vision, while the continued involvement of former CEO Danny Prosky as a director and advisor supports a smooth transition.

The promotion of Gabe Willhite to President, while retaining his COO responsibilities, and the designation of Scott Estes as Lead Independent Director, signal a deep and experienced management bench that can accelerate execution without altering the company’s strategic direction. Kamdem views these appointments as constructive for scaling the platform with limited operational disruption, supporting sustained growth and an enhanced focus on quality of care, which underpins his positive stance on the stock.

In another report released on July 21, Compass Point also initiated coverage with a Buy rating on the stock with a $70.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on Prothena, Maintains $30 Price Target on Differentiated TTR-Depleter Opportunity
data.description:
Analyst Andrew Fein from H.C. Wainwright maintained a Buy rating on Prothena and keeping the price target at $30.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Andrew Fein has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to the growing validation of TTR-depleter therapies and Prothena’s differentiated position within this space. He views the recent Royalty Pharma–Neurimmune transaction as external confirmation that depleter economics are attractive, with robust imaging and biomarker data suggesting a disease-modifying mechanism that existing stabilizers and silencers have yet to match.

In his view, Prothena’s partner Novo’s coramitug program exhibits meaningful clinical differentiation, including the only placebo-controlled Phase 2 dataset for a TTR depleter and strong NT-proBNP reductions on top of tafamidis background therapy. Combined with roughly $1.08B in remaining Novo milestones, tiered royalties on a multibillion-dollar market, and a modestly valued stock that he believes does not reflect this upside, Fein maintains a Buy rating with an unchanged $30 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on TotalEnergies with Unchanged $82 Target, Citing Earnings Resilience and Strong Future Cash Flows
data.description:
In a report released today, Christopher Kuplent from Bank of America Securities reiterated a Buy rating on TotalEnergies SE, with a price target of €82.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Christopher Kuplent has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight both earnings resilience and cash flow strength. He views TotalEnergies as his preferred name among the major oil companies and notes that the company’s second-quarter results underscored the benefits of its broad, integrated portfolio, even with a portion of production constrained by geopolitical bottlenecks.

Kuplent also emphasizes that his forecasts still imply meaningful upside to market expectations for 2026 cash flow and returns, supporting a continued Buy stance with an unchanged $82 price target. In his view, TotalEnergies offers an attractive total distribution yield around 8% in 2026 and is poised for strong free cash flow generation toward 2028, outperforming peers under mid-cycle commodity price assumptions.

In another report released today, UBS also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a €86.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
